sentences.text: This chapter defines the "Zfinx" extension (pronounced "z-f-in-x") that provides instructions similar to those in the standard floating-point F extension for single-precision floating-point instructions but which operate on the `x` registers instead of the `f` registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: This chapter also defines the "Zdinx", "Zhinx", and "Zhinxmin" extensions that provide similar instructions for other floating-point precisions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Zfinx extension adds all of the instructions that the F extension adds, except for the transfer instructions FLW, FSW, FMV.W.X, FMV.X.W, C.FLW[SP], and C.FSW[SP].
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Zfinx variants of these F-extension instructions have the same semantics, except that whenever such an instruction would have accessed an `f` register, it instead accesses the `x` register with the same number.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Zfinx extension depends on the "Zicsr" extension for control and status register access.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Floating-point operands of width w < XLEN bits occupy bits w-1:0 of an `x` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: Floating-point operations on w-bit operands ignore operand bits XLEN-1: w.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: Floating-point operations that produce w < XLEN-bit results fill bits XLEN-1: w with copies of bit w-1 (the sign bit).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: The Zdinx extension provides analogous double-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zdinx

sentences.text: The Zdinx extension depends upon the Zfinx extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zdinx

sentences.text: The Zdinx extension adds all of the instructions that the D extension adds, except for the transfer instructions FLD, FSD, FMV.D.X, FMV.X.D, C.FLD[SP], and C.FSD[SP].
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zdinx

sentences.text: The Zdinx variants of these D-extension instructions have the same semantics, except that whenever such an instruction would have accessed an `f` register, it instead accesses the `x` register with the same number.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zdinx

sentences.text: Double-precision operands in RV32Zdinx are held in aligned `x`-register pairs, i.e., register numbers must be even.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Processing of Wider Values

sentences.text: Use of misaligned (odd-numbered) registers for double-width floating-point operands is reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Processing of Wider Values

sentences.text: Regardless of endianness, the lower-numbered register holds the low-order bits, and the higher-numbered register holds the high-order bits: e.g., bits 31:0 of a double-precision operand in RV32Zdinx might be held in register `x14`, with bits 63:32 of that operand held in `x15`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Processing of Wider Values

sentences.text: When a double-width floating-point result is written to `x0`, the entire write takes no effect: e.g., for RV32Zdinx, writing a double-precision result to `x0` does not cause `x1` to be written.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Processing of Wider Values

sentences.text: When `x0` is used as a double-width floating-point operand, the entire operand is zero—i.e., `x1` is not accessed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Processing of Wider Values

sentences.text: The Zhinx extension provides analogous half-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinx

sentences.text: The Zhinx extension depends upon the Zfinx extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinx

sentences.text: The Zhinx extension adds all of the instructions that the Zfh extension adds, except for the transfer instructions FLH, FSH, FMV.H.X, and FMV.X.H.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinx

sentences.text: The Zhinx variants of these Zfh-extension instructions have the same semantics, except that whenever such an instruction would have accessed an `f` register, it instead accesses the `x` register with the same number.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinx

sentences.text: The Zhinxmin extension provides minimal support for 16-bit half-precision floating-point instructions that operate on the `x` registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinxmin

sentences.text: The Zhinxmin extension depends upon the Zfinx extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinxmin

sentences.text: The Zhinxmin extension includes the following instructions from the Zhinx extension: FCVT.S.H and FCVT.H.S.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinxmin

sentences.text: If the Zdinx extension is present, the FCVT.D.H and FCVT.H.D instructions are also included.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Zhinxmin

sentences.text: As described in , the `mstatus` field FS is hardwired to 0 if the Zfinx extension is implemented, and FS no longer affects the trapping behavior of floating-point instructions or `fcsr` accesses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Privileged Architecture Implications

sentences.text: The `misa` bits F, D, and Q are hardwired to 0 when the Zfinx extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zfinx", "Zdinx", "Zhinx", "Zhinxmin" Extensions for Floating-Point in Integer Registers, Version 1.0 > Processing of Narrower Values > Privileged Architecture Implications
